vulnerability.title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the kernel.randomize_va_space kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w kernel.randomize_va_space=2 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of kernel.randomize_va_space from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*kernel.randomize_va_space.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "kernel.randomize_va_space" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl config f

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves setting a sysctl parameter, which is a low-risk change that is unlikely to lock out access.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_056 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_kernel_randomize_va_space). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message: The process outlined by the tools provided does not account for failures that occur during their execution, and there isn't an adequate resolution path provided. To accomplish the goal specified there are certain modifications required. As a direct continuation, further commands would likely revolve around closely inspecting the results of previous operations and deriving future steps based on that analysis.

review_feedback: The remediation script contains redundant and error-prone steps, fails to cleanly handle all sysctl files, and does not verify the sysctl change succeeded after apply, which could lead to unexpected behavior or failures Suggestions: Simply add 'kernel.randomize_va_space = 2' to a dedicated sysctl.d file and reload; Use a single sysctl -p after writing the file, without intermediate base64 gymnastics; Validate the parameter with sysctl -n kernel.randomize_va_space after applying; Remove the unrelated net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians line unless required

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation attempts to enable kernel.randomize_va_space=2 and make it persistent, but the script contains redundant and error-prone steps and does not cleanly handle all sysctl files. It would work if corrected, but as written it fails.
concerns: Redundant sysctl -w calls and unnecessary echo of unrelated parameters
Use of base64 decoding for a simple file write is convoluted and error-prone
Only comments out exact matches, potentially leaving stray whitespace or comments
Does not verify the sysctl change succeeded after apply
Hard-codes the config file path without checking for existing entries properly
suggested_improvements: Simply add 'kernel.randomize_va_space = 2' to a dedicated sysctl.d file and reload
Use a single sysctl -p after writing the file, without intermediate base64 gymnastics
Validate the parameter with sysctl -n kernel.randomize_va_space after applying
Remove the unrelated net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians line unless required
